comment_text.sn3.3:1.3: The subtext is that once one has obtained kingship, immortality becomes tempting.
comment_text.sn3.3:2.3: These two lines contain three forms of the same word santa, “a good person”: sataṁ is genitive plural; santo is nominative plural; sabbhi is dative plural (following Brough and Norman).

root_text.sn3.3:1.2: Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho rājā pasenadi kosalo bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn3.3:1.3: “atthi nu kho, bhante, jātassa aññatra jarāmaraṇā”ti?
root_text.sn3.3:1.5: Yepi te, mahārāja, khattiyamahāsālā aḍḍhā mahaddhanā mahābhogā pahūtajātarūparajatā pahūtavittūpakaraṇā pahūtadhanadhaññā, tesampi jātānaṁ natthi aññatra jarāmaraṇā.
root_text.sn3.3:1.7: gahapatimahāsālā aḍḍhā mahaddhanā mahābhogā pahūtajātarūparajatā pahūtavittūpakaraṇā pahūtadhanadhaññā, tesampi jātānaṁ natthi aññatra jarāmaraṇā.
root_text.sn3.3:1.8: Yepi te, mahārāja, bhikkhū arahanto khīṇāsavā vusitavanto katakaraṇīyā ohitabhārā anuppattasadatthā parikkhīṇabhavasaṁyojanā sammadaññāvimuttā, tesampāyaṁ kāyo bhedanadhammo nikkhepanadhammo”ti.

translation_text.sn3.3:1.2: Seated to one side, King Pasenadi said to the Buddha,
translation_text.sn3.3:1.3: “Sir, for someone who has been reborn, is there any exemption from old age and death?”
translation_text.sn3.3:1.4: “Great king, for someone who has been reborn, there’s no exemption from old age and death.
translation_text.sn3.3:1.7: or householders—rich, affluent, and wealthy, with lots of gold and silver, lots of property and assets, and lots of money and grain—when they’re born, there’s no exemption from old age and death.
translation_text.sn3.3:1.8: Even for mendicants who are perfected—who have ended the defilements, completed the spiritual journey, done what had to be done, laid down the burden, achieved their heart’s goal, utterly ended the fetter of continued existence, and are rightly freed through enlightenment—their bodies are liable to break up and be laid to rest.”
translation_text.sn3.3:1.9: That is what the Buddha said. …
translation_text.sn3.3:2.2: and even this body gets old.
translation_text.sn3.3:2.3: But the truth of the good never gets old—
translation_text.sn3.3:2.4: so the good proclaim to the good.”

reference_text.sn3.3:1.1: cck15.97, csp1ed12.71, csp2ed12.71, dr15.97, ms12S1_594, msdiv114, ndp12.70, pts-vp-pli1ed1.71, pts-vp-pli2ed1.164
